ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic hamburger class (FOODON_00002473). The Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast is a shrimp-patty fast-food burger set meal; no exact FoodOn class exists for this composite feast item. Closest defensible anchor used.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0FJP covers burger/hamburger-type products. The feast format (burger + fries + beverage + pie) is a composite meal not individually coded in FoodEx2; the burger component anchor is used as the primary ontology reference.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT concept for shrimp burger fast-food feast meal. SNOMED CT clinical food coding does not extend to branded composite fast-food sets.

serving.description: 1 feast set (burger + M fries + S Coke Zero + pie)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard feast set comprising one Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger, one medium serving of French fries, one small Coke Zero, and one pie, totalling approximately 570 g as reported by HPB Singapore.

nutrient_source.title: Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast — HPB Singapore Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for the Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast (cr_id G00010106). All per-100g values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat, monounsaturated fat, polyunsaturated fat, trans fat, and several micronutrients not reported by HPB are set to -1 (data not available).

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for the Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast composite meal. GI of 72 is estimated using the analogous fast-food burger meal anchor from the University of Sydney GI Database (hamburger on white bun GI ~66–72; French fries GI ~63–75; composite meal weighted estimate ~72). The feast includes a diet cola (Coke Zero, negligible GI contribution) and a pie (pastry, GI ~59). The dominant glycaemic drivers are the white-flour bun and deep-fried French fries. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g_per_serving) / 100 = (72 × 167.5) / 100 ≈ 120.5, where carbohydrate per 570 g serving = 29.57 × 5.7 = 168.5 g (rounded to 167.5 g for conservative estimate). This is a high-GL meal.

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium content (487 mg/100 g) with low potassium offset (197 mg/100 g), compounded by additional sodium from seasoned French fries and spicy tomato sauce in the feast format
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid this feast meal due to its high sodium load (~2,776 mg per 570 g serving, exceeding the 2,000 mg daily limit in a single meal); if consumed, request no added salt on fries, reduce sauce portions, and replace any sugar-sweetened beverage with water.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~72) and very high glycaemic load per serving (~120.5) driven by refined white-flour bun, deep-fried high-GI French fries, and 29.57 g carbohydrate per 100 g with only 1.8 g dietary fibre
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid this feast meal; the combined glycaemic load of the white-flour bun, deep-fried fries, and pastry pie is very high, and if consumed at all, the fries should be substituted with a side salad, the pie omitted, and portion size strictly limited to no more than 150 g.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Ultra-processed deep-fried fast-food meal with high sodium (487 mg/100 g), cholesterol (29 mg/100 g), and likely saturated and trans fat from deep-frying of both the ebi patty and French fries
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit or avoid this meal due to its high sodium content, deep-fried preparation generating oxidised lipids and likely trans fats, and ultra-processed composition; if consumed occasionally, portion should not exceed 150 g and should be paired with a large serving of vegetables.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-fried preparation of both ebi patty and French fries generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); tomato sauce contains nightshade solanine; refined sugar from feast components promotes pro-inflammatory mediators
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid this meal entirely, particularly during flares, as deep-fried AGE-generating components, nightshade tomato sauce, and refined carbohydrates collectively promote systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast is a Singapore fast-food set meal offered by a major quick-service restaurant chain. The feast comprises four components: (1) a Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger — a deep-fried shrimp (ebi) patty served on a toasted white-flour bun with spicy tomato sauce, lettuce, and mayonnaise; (2) a medium serving of deep-fried French fries; (3) a small Coke Zero (diet cola, negligible caloric contribution); and (4) a baked pastry pie. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is prepared primarily by deep-frying. Per 100 g, the feast delivers 263 kcal, 13.3 g protein, 10.18 g fat, and 29.57 g carbohydrate with only 1.8 g dietary fibre, making it a high-energy, high-carbohydrate, moderate-protein meal with a poor fibre-to-carbohydrate ratio. Sodium is elevated at 487 mg/100 g, yielding approximately 2,776 mg per 570 g serving — exceeding the recommended daily sodium intake in a single meal. The glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high), driven by the refined white-flour bun and deep-fried fries, resulting in a very high glycaemic load per serving (~120.5). The meal is not suitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. It is a popular lunch and dinner option in Singapore, reflecting the localisation of Western fast-food culture with Southeast Asian seafood preferences (ebi = shrimp in Japanese, widely used in Singapore fast-food menus). Allergens include gluten, crustaceans, eggs, and potentially soy and dairy. The meal is not vegetarian, vegan, or halal-certified by default.

confidence_reason: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010106, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. All per-100g macros and available micronutrients are taken verbatim from the HPB record. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database as no direct GI measurement exists for this composite feast meal.
notes: saturated_fat_g, monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown for the Spicy Tomato Ebi Burger Feast is not reported in the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry (cr_id G00010106) as of 2026-04-16. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, water_g set to -1: these micronutrient and moisture values are not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this item as of 2026-04-16. The input record noted trans fat presence (0.1 g/100 g) in the health_suitability section; however, this value is not confirmed in the HPB per_100g data block and has therefore been set to -1 pending source confirmation. The feast description includes Coke Zero (diet cola, not a sugar-sweetened beverage), which modifies the diabetes and hypertension SSB assessment compared to a standard cola feast — the per-100g composite values already reflect the Coke Zero component. Halal status is not confirmed in the HPB record; is_halal set to false pending certification verification. GI of 72 is an analogous estimate; direct GI measurement for this composite meal is not available in the University of Sydney GI Database as of 2026-04-16.
